system_description.overview: FullEnrich is a B2B contact enrichment platform that aggregates data from over 15 premium vendors to provide accurate and up-to-date email addresses and phone numbers for prospects. It is a SaaS platform for enriching business data, streamlining operations, and integrating with third-party CRM and marketing automation tools.

products.Data Enrichment Tools: Delivering accurate, comprehensive, and actionable data to improve customer insights, targeting, and personalization
products.Workflow Automation Platforms: Enabling businesses to optimize processes, reduce manual work, and increase operational efficiency
products.Customer Profiling Solutions: Building detailed and dynamic customer profiles that support personalized experiences and smarter decision-making
products.Integration Services: Connecting systems and platforms seamlessly to ensure data consistency, real-time insights, and scalable growth
products.Training & Enablement: Equip teams with the knowledge and tools to maximize product value
products.Strategic Consulting: Provide tailored guidance to improve operations, optimize processes, and scale growth

regions: Not specifically named; DigitalOcean data centers
infrastructure.physical_presence: No physical offices in scope; 166 Geary Street SF is mailing address only. All data processed on cloud services.

network_architecture.network_segmentation: Segmented VPCs with access control lists (ACLs). Network segmentation implemented to separate systems and data based on sensitivity and criticality.
network_architecture.api_gateway: Application gateway mentioned in firewall context (CC6.6.1: firewalls configured on the application gateway)
network_architecture.firewalls: System firewalls configured on the application gateway and production network to limit unnecessary ports, protocols and services. Firewall rules reviewed annually by IT management. Firewall access restricted to authorized personnel.
network_architecture.ids_ips: IDS and IPS employed via DigitalOcean in conjunction with security policy network settings
network_architecture.vpn: Remote access done through a bastion host with a virtual private network (VPN)
network_architecture.tls: TLS using certificates from an established third-party certificate authority for all web-based systems
network_architecture.content_filtering: Systems restricted to internet with content filtering system routed through proxy server
network_architecture.topology: Users -> HTTPS -> Project FullEnrich (prod/dev) -> Availability Zone FRA1 -> Services (HTTP Service 1, Application Load Balancer, HTTP Service 2, Worker Service) -> Database (Postgres) + Cache (Redis). Metrics layer: Prometheus + Grafana -> LogStash (Logs) + Tempo (Traces). Separate prod and dev environments with identical architecture.

application_architecture.environments: Two separate environments: Production (Project FullEnrich-prod) and Development (Project FullEnrich-dev), identically architected. Changes developed and tested in separate dev/test environment before production (CC8.1.4).

authentication_access_control.identity_provider: DigitalOcean IAM / internal authentication (not specifically named as third-party IdP)
authentication_access_control.mfa: MFA enforced for user accounts with administrative access to production platform (CC6.1.1). MFA enforced for all remote access. MFA required for source code tool access (CC8.1.3). Two-factor authentication required for employee email access.
authentication_access_control.password_policy: Controlled through DigitalOcean password policy including periodic forced changes, password expiry and complexity requirements. Accounts disabled after limited number of unsuccessful logon attempts. Guest and anonymous logins not allowed. Desktops lock within a time of inactivity.

encryption.at_rest: Cloud provider key management service used to encrypt data at rest (CC6.1.5)
encryption.key_management: DigitalOcean managed key management service. Access to encryption keys restricted to authorized personnel only (CC6.1.9).

ci_cd_devops.source_control: Source code management tool (not named; likely Git-based given branch protection references)
ci_cd_devops.branch_protection: Repository branch rules configured to ensure every merge request to move change to production environment requires additional approval (CC8.1.2). Developers cannot make changes to production without additional approval.
ci_cd_devops.iac: Baseline configurations retained within infrastructure as code. Configuration management tool used for implementation and rollback capability (CC7.1.1).
ci_cd_devops.patch_management: CTO/Management set up automatic patch management with monthly patch management reviews. Workstations configured for automatic updates and scanned for patch compliance daily.

monitoring_logging.alerting: Automated alerts configured to notify IT management of high and critical risk events. Monitoring tool generates alerts when specific predefined thresholds are met.

third_party_services.purpose: Cloud infrastructure / data center services (subservice organization)

security_tools.antivirus: Anti-virus and Malware protection software installed on all production servers and Mac-based workstations/laptops. Auto-scan continuous. Production server definitions updated weekly.
security_tools.vulnerability_scanning: Monthly automated vulnerability scans on infrastructure and applications (CC7.1.2). Self-assessment scan of vulnerabilities performed prior to every release to production or on yearly basis.
security_tools.penetration_testing: Annual external penetration testing by independent third-party. Results reviewed by IT management. High/critical findings remediated in timely manner (CC4.1.1).

untestable: CC2.2.3 - Significant changes to people, roles and responsibilities could not be tested because there were no significant changes made during the engagement period
CC3.2.1 - Cybersecurity insurance could not be tested because there were no cyber incidents reported during the engagement period
CC6.5.2 - Customer data deletion upon termination could not be tested because there were no customer terminations during the engagement period
CC7.3.1 - Incident response could not be tested because there were no security incidents reported during the engagement period
excluded_criteria: CC6.4 - Physical access to facilities: DigitalOcean responsible for physical security of data center; not in scope
A1.2 - Environmental protections, software, data backup processes, and recovery infrastructure: No office; managed by building management / DigitalOcean
Processing Integrity - Determined not relevant to the system
Privacy - Determined not relevant to the system
notable_controls: CC8.1.2 - Branch protection with merge request approval required for production changes
CC7.1.1 - Infrastructure as code with configuration management tool and rollback capability
CC7.4.1 - BCDR policy documented and reviewed annually with testing of different loss scenarios
CC7.4.2 - Daily backups with multi-AZ configuration and alerts for backup failures
CC6.1.7 - Network segmentation based on sensitivity and criticality
CC2.1.1 - Intrusion detection system utilized to monitor and detect unauthorized access
CC4.1.1 - Annual external penetration testing by independent third-party
CC6.1.10 - Data encryption at rest and in transit

infrastructure.rationale: Single cloud provider (DigitalOcean) with multi-AZ but no multi-region. VPC with segmentation is good. No CDN mentioned. IaC with rollback is positive. However, DigitalOcean is a less mature enterprise cloud compared to AWS/GCP/Azure, and no specific regions are disclosed. No serverless or container orchestration mentioned.

application_architecture.rationale: Microservices pattern evident from network diagram (multiple HTTP services, worker service, load balancer). Separate prod/dev environments with identical architecture. However, no programming languages, frameworks, or API styles explicitly named. Branch protection and code review are confirmed. No mention of automated testing.

data_layer.rationale: PostgreSQL + Redis is a solid combination. Daily backups with multi-AZ. Data classification policy and retention policy in place. Per-tenant segregation confirmed (CC6.1.3). However, no data warehouse, no file storage details, and backup frequency is only daily (not hourly).

security_posture.rationale: Strong security controls: MFA enforced, IDS/IPS, firewalls with annual review, annual external pen testing, monthly vulnerability scanning, endpoint protection with antivirus, server hardening, cybersecurity insurance, encryption at rest and in transit. Network segmentation with ACLs. Content filtering via proxy. VPN for remote access. However, no WAF mentioned, no named SIEM tool, and TLS version not specified.

devops_maturity.rationale: IaC with configuration management and rollback confirmed. Branch protection with merge approval required. Separate environments. Emergency change process documented. Formal change management policy. However, no CI/CD tool specifically named, no container orchestration mentioned, source control tool unnamed. Patch management is monthly rather than continuous.

bcdr_readiness.rationale: BCDR policy documented and reviewed annually. Annual testing of different loss scenarios. Daily backups with multi-AZ. Multiple servers across all availability zones in DigitalOcean for failover. However, no multi-region failover, RTO and RPO not disclosed, and no specific recovery time targets mentioned.

vendor_diversity.rationale: Heavy single-vendor dependency on DigitalOcean for all infrastructure. Monitoring stack (Prometheus/Grafana/LogStash/Tempo) is open-source which is good for portability. 15+ data vendors for enrichment is diverse on the data side. But infrastructure is entirely concentrated in one cloud provider with no CDN or additional cloud services.

overall.rationale: FullEnrich Corp demonstrates a baseline-to-moderate technology posture for a B2B SaaS company. Core security controls are well-implemented (MFA, encryption, IDS/IPS, pen testing, vulnerability scanning). The microservices architecture with PostgreSQL/Redis and multi-AZ is reasonable. However, the report lacks specificity on many tech details (no named languages, frameworks, CI/CD tools, or cloud regions). Single-vendor cloud dependency on DigitalOcean and no multi-region capability are concerns. The short 3-month audit period and several untestable controls due to no incidents/changes during the period are notable. Clean audit with zero exceptions is positive.

red_flags: Single cloud provider dependency (DigitalOcean) with no multi-region failover capability
RTO and RPO not disclosed despite having a BCDR policy
Very short audit period (3 months: April 25 - July 25, 2025) for a Type 2 report
Several controls untestable due to no events during the short engagement period (incident response, cybersecurity insurance, customer data deletion, personnel changes)
No WAF (Web Application Firewall) mentioned for a web-based SaaS platform
TLS version not specified - could be using older versions
yellow_flags: No specific programming languages, frameworks, or CI/CD tools named throughout the report - suggests templated/generic report content
No named SIEM tool for security event management
No container orchestration mentioned despite microservices architecture
DigitalOcean is a less enterprise-grade cloud provider compared to AWS/GCP/Azure
No CDN mentioned for a customer-facing SaaS platform
Processing Integrity and Privacy trust criteria excluded - notable for a data enrichment company handling B2B contact information
Cybersecurity insurance control could not be verified operationally (no incidents to test against)
Backup frequency only daily - potential for up to 24 hours of data loss
green_flags: Clean unqualified opinion with zero exceptions across all tested controls
MFA enforced for production access, remote access, source code access, and email
Annual external penetration testing by independent third-party
Monthly automated vulnerability scanning with remediation tracking
Infrastructure as code with rollback capability
Branch protection requiring merge approval for production deployments
Formal BCDR policy with annual testing of different loss scenarios
Multi-AZ deployment with failover capability across availability zones
Data classification policy established and implemented
Network segmentation based on sensitivity and criticality with VPC and ACLs
Formal vendor management program with annual SOC report reviews and quarterly risk assessments
Daily backups with multi-AZ configuration and alerting on failures
Endpoint protection with antivirus on servers and workstations
Open-source monitoring stack (Prometheus/Grafana/LogStash/Tempo) providing good observability
key_observations: The report is notably generic/templated in several sections - many control descriptions are boilerplate rather than specific to FullEnrich's actual implementation. This reduces the technical intelligence extractable from the report.

Network diagram on page 19 reveals the most specific technical detail: microservices architecture with load balancer, Postgres, Redis, and a Prometheus/Grafana/LogStash/Tempo observability stack, all in DigitalOcean FRA1 (Frankfurt) region.

The FRA1 region label in the network diagram suggests the company runs in European data center despite being headquartered in San Francisco - potentially for GDPR compliance given B2B contact data handling.

Privacy and Processing Integrity are excluded from trust criteria despite the company being a B2B data enrichment platform that aggregates data from 15+ vendors - this is a notable gap for due diligence purposes.
The 3-month audit period is unusually short for a SOC 2 Type 2 (typical is 6-12 months), which may indicate this is their first SOC 2 audit.
The org chart on page 21 shows a relatively flat structure with CEO Benjamin Douablin at top, suggesting a smaller organization.
The report mentions 'Windows/Network team' for patch management (page 20) which is unusual for a cloud-native SaaS company and may indicate legacy infrastructure elements or a templated section.
